name: Vecty
description: React-like library for building web frontends in Go using WebAssembly with minimal dependencies

description.value: React-like library for building web frontends in Go using WebAssembly with minimal dependencies

website.comment: No dedicated website; GitHub repository serves as primary documentation

initial-release.comment: Repository history shows development starting circa 2017; project has been in development over 4 years per docs

latest-release.comment: No formal GitHub releases published; uses Go module versioning (v0.6.0 on pkg.go.dev)

current-version.comment: v0.6.0 per pkg.go.dev; no official GitHub releases

active-maintenance.comment: Last meaningful commit was October 2022; no activity in 2023, 2024, or 2025; project is dormant

reactivity-model.comment: Fast expectation-based DOM diffing (virtual DOM with less resource usage); requires explicit Rerender() calls

component-model.comment: Components are Go structs embedding vecty.Core; implement Render() method returning ComponentOrHTML

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative component tree built with elem and prop packages; similar to React's JSX but with Go function calls

state-management-approach.comment: Component-level state via struct fields; updates trigger manual vecty.Rerender() calls

opinionatedness.comment: Minimal framework asking 'What is the best way to solve this problem in Go?' rather than translating other libraries

backward-compatibility.comment: Pre-v1.0.0 experimental project; docs explicitly state APIs will change (maybe extensively)

primary-language.comment: 100% Go; nearly zero dependencies, only relies on reflect from Go stdlib

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly (via standard Go compiler) and vanilla JavaScript (via GopherJS)

typescript-support.comment: Go framework; uses Go's type system instead of TypeScript

template-syntax.comment: Go DSL using elem and prop packages; function calls like elem.Div(elem.H1(vecty.Text("Hello")))

styling-approach.comment: vecty.Style() for inline styles; vecty.Class() for CSS classes; no built-in scoping

build-required.comment: Requires Go 1.14+ with GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm; wasmserve recommended for development

wasm-interop.comment: Uses syscall/js for WASM interop; SyscallJSValue type for testing without browser

reactive-primitives.comment: No built-in reactive primitives; state changes require manual Rerender() calls

computed-values.comment: No automatic computed values; derived state must be calculated in Render() or component methods

two-way-binding.comment: No built-in two-way binding; manual event handling and state updates required

event-handling.comment: Declarative event binding via event package (e.g., event.Input, event.Click with callback functions)

conditional-rendering.comment: Standard Go conditionals (if statements) in Render() method; vecty.If() helper available

list-rendering.comment: Go for loops in Render(); vecty.List type for returning multiple elements

component-slots.comment: Children passed as ComponentOrHTML to component Render methods

suspense.comment: No built-in suspense; async operations handled via goroutines with manual rerendering

context-provide-inject.comment: No built-in context/dependency injection; can pass via component struct fields

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM access via syscall/js package; RenderInto targets specific CSS selectors

official-router.comment: No official router; URL-based routing listed as pre-v1.0.0 goal not yet ready

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No official router; WASM binary compiled as single unit

built-in-state.comment: Component state via Go struct fields; manual Rerender() to propagate changes

built-in-global-state.comment: No built-in global state solution; can use shared Go package variables or pass through props

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No browser devtools for state inspection; debugging via standard Go/WASM tools

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; can use browser storage APIs manually via syscall/js

official-cli.comment: No official CLI; recommends wasmserve for development; vectytemplater is community tool

hot-module-replacement.comment: No HMR; wasmserve provides development server but requires manual refresh

browser-devtools.comment: No Vecty-specific browser extension; relies on standard WebAssembly debugging

vs-code-extension.comment: No Vecty-specific VS Code extension; uses standard Go tooling

error-messages.comment: Go compiler errors are helpful with type safety; designed to be easily mastered by newcomers

documentation-quality.comment: Basic README and pkg.go.dev reference; extensive docs listed as pre-v1.0.0 goal not yet ready; community-maintained vectydoc exists

interactive-tutorial.comment: No interactive tutorial; examples in repository

playground.comment: No online playground due to Go/WASM compilation requirements

bundle-size-core.comment: ~587KB gzipped for hello world with Go 1.15; minimal dependencies (only reflect from stdlib)

bundle-size-full.comment: ~1MB gzipped for markdown app example; TodoMVC is 825KB gzipped

virtual-dom.comment: Uses expectation-based DOM diffing (virtual DOM approach but with less resource usage)

compile-time-optimization.comment: Standard Go compiler optimizations; TinyGo support tracked in issue #269

tree-shaking.comment: Go compiler eliminates unused code; framework has nearly zero dependencies

code-splitting.comment: No WASM binary splitting; entire app compiles to single WASM file

lazy-components.comment: No lazy component loading; all components part of single WASM binary

ssr-capable.comment: Server-side rendering listed as pre-v1.0.0 goal not yet ready

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in js-framework-benchmark; Go/WASM framework with limited benchmark data

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Go module, not npm package; distributed via Go modules (go get github.com/hexops/vecty)

component-libraries.comment: soypat/mdc (Material Design), vecty-components/material, wizenerd/ui (Material Design Lite)

meta-framework.comment: No meta-framework; designed for browser-based WebAssembly apps only

major-companies-using.comment: No publicly documented production usage by major companies; experimental project used by hobbyists (Go Package Store, Go Play Space)

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven project under hexops; previously under gopherjs and gowasm orgs

discord-community-size.comment: Uses Slack (#gopherjs and #vecty channels on Gophers Slack) instead of Discord

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Very few Stack Overflow questions; no dedicated tag; community primarily uses GitHub issues and Slack

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; experimental Go/WASM framework with minimal job market presence; dormant development

official-testing-library.comment: SyscallJSValue allows testing Vecty under native 'go test' without browser

testing-library-name.comment: Standard Go testing with SyscallJSValue for browser-less component testing

component-testing.comment: Components can be tested via native Go tests using SyscallJSValue

e2e-testing-integration.comment: No documented E2E testing integration; wasmbrowsertest can be used for browser tests

snapshot-testing.comment: No built-in snapshot testing; would require manual implementation

mobile-framework.comment: No official mobile framework; browser-only WebAssembly target

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA support; would require manual service worker configuration

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; could theoretically work with Electron or Tauri

learning-curve.comment: Moderate; requires Go knowledge; React-like patterns are familiar; designed to be easily mastered by newcomers

concepts-to-learn.comment: Go basics, vecty.Core embedding, Render() method, elem/prop packages, event handling, WASM build process

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes with Go installed; wasmserve provides quick development setup

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from JS frameworks; requires rewrite in Go

incremental-adoption.comment: RenderInto allows targeting specific CSS selectors; WASM can be integrated into existing websites
